item.title: We have to be more patient and find balance - Ikanivere after 39-24 loss to Wales
item.description: We have to be patient more, says FIJI Water Flying Fijians captain Tevita Ikanivere, as the side reflects on key decision-making issues following their 39–24 defeat to Wales in Cardiff. Speaking during the post-match press conference, Ikanivere says Fiji’s attacking style is developing, but the team must find the right balance between fast, expansive play and controlled phases, particularly in sco
item.content:encoded:
We have to be patient more, says FIJI Water Flying Fijians captain Tevita Ikanivere, as the side reflects on key decision-making issues following their 39–24 defeat to Wales in Cardiff.

Speaking during the post-match press conference, Ikanivere says Fiji’s attacking style is developing, but the team must find the right balance between fast, expansive play and controlled phases, particularly in scoring positions.

Insert: Ikanivere - Need to find that balance July 05

Ikanivere says the team must learn when to slow the game down and when to speed it up in order to become more effective in crucial moments.

Despite the loss, the captain pointed to encouraging attacking statistics, including 667 metres made, 23 clean breaks and 42 offloads, but admitted they were not converted into enough points.

He says execution under pressure remains a key focus area, particularly when deciding whether to offload or take the ball into contact.

Fiji will now turn its attention to its next Test as it looks to bounce back from the opening defeat.

Fiji will face England on Sunday at 1.10am at Hill Dickinson Stadium in Liverpool, England.

item.title: EPL: Labasa climb to the top after 3-1 win over Nadroga
item.description: Labasa FC has climbed to the top of the Extra Premier League standings after producing a stunning comeback to beat Nadroga FC 3-1. Nadroga struck first and carried a 1-0 lead into the half-time break, putting the Babasiga Lions under pressure.
item.content:encoded:
Labasa FC has climbed to the top of the Extra Premier League standings after producing a stunning comeback to beat Nadroga FC 3-1.

Nadroga struck first and carried a 1-0 lead into the half-time break, putting the Babasiga Lions under pressure.

However, Labasa responded in style after the restart, scoring three unanswered goals to complete a memorable turnaround and claim all three points.

Meanwhile, in other results, Rewa FC thrashed Lautoka FC 4-0, Nasinu FC beat Tailevu Naitasiri 3-1, Ba FC and Nadi FC battled to a hard-fought 1-1 draw, while Navua FC and Suva FC also shared the spoils in an entertaining 2-2 draw.

item.title: Rewa thrash Lautoka 4-0 at Churchill Park
item.description: Rewa FC produced a spirited performance at Churchill Park to defeat Lautoka FC 4-0 in the Extra Premier League, showing great fight and composure despite playing most of the match with 10 men. The Delta Tigers got off to a perfect start, with Don Keana striking in the fourth minute to put Rewa in front and set the tone for the contest.
item.content:encoded:
Rewa FC produced a spirited performance at Churchill Park to defeat Lautoka FC 4-0 in the Extra Premier League, showing great fight and composure despite playing most of the match with 10 men.

The Delta Tigers got off to a perfect start, with Don Keana striking in the fourth minute to put Rewa in front and set the tone for the contest.

Their task became tougher in the 35th minute when Ivan Kumar was shown a red card, reducing Rewa to 10 men for the remainder of the match.

However, Rewa refused to back down, with Josaia Sela extending the lead before Gabriele Matanisiga and Epeli Valevou added two more goals to seal an impressive 4-0 victory.

Meanwhile, Nasinu FC beat Tailevu Naitasiri 3-1.

item.title: Funding shortfall threatens Fiji's historic World Handball debut in Romania
item.description: Fiji's historic debut at the International Handball Federation (IHF) Women's Youth World Championship is under threat as players and their families race to raise the funds needed to get the national Under-18 handball team to Romania next month. The 12-member squad is preparing to become the first team from the Pacific Islands to compete at the IHF Women's Youth World Championship, which will be he
item.content:encoded:
Fiji's historic debut at the International Handball Federation (IHF) Women's Youth World Championship is under threat as players and their families race to raise the funds needed to get the national Under-18 handball team to Romania next month.

The 12-member squad is preparing to become the first team from the Pacific Islands to compete at the IHF Women's Youth World Championship, which will be held in Romania from 29 July to 9 August.

However, a parent of one of the players says uncertainty over funding has left families scrambling to cover travel costs despite earlier discussions about financial assistance.

The parent says the Fiji Sports Commission had initially been expected to cover the bulk of the team's expenses, but the Federation was later informed that providing the funding would be difficult.

They say they are unsure why the funding did not eventuate, although it coincided with the end of the financial year and the lead-up to the National Budget.

According to the parent, accommodation support has since eased some of the financial burden, with China offering to cover the team's week-long stay in Hong Kong during transit and organisers in Romania are expected to provide accommodation during the championship.

The parent says the main challenge now is raising enough money to pay for airfares, local transport, uniforms and contingency expenses.

The parent adds that families initially faced costs of around $13,000 per player, although that amount has since reduced following the accommodation support.

Parents have continued fundraising individually while the Fiji Handball Federation seeks sponsorship from businesses and other organisations.

A fundraising event has also been organised this week to help meet the remaining costs, while the Federation continues discussions with potential sponsors and remains hopeful additional support may still be secured.

He says the team must finalise visa applications, insurance and other travel documentation by next Friday to ensure they can participate in the world championship.

The parent adds the tournament represents a landmark moment not only for Fiji but for the Pacific region, as it is expected to be the first time a Pacific Island nation has qualified for the IHF Women's Youth World Championship.

Meanwhile, we have sent questions to the Women's U18 Handball Fiji Team coach Lusi Rokoura, and the Fiji Sports Commission.

item.title: Mbappé penalty sends France into World Cup quarter-finals after 1-0 win over Paraguay
item.description: France have booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals after a hard-fought 1-0 win over Paraguay in Philadelphia, with Kylian Mbappé’s second-half penalty proving decisive. The World Cup favourites were made to work for their victory as Paraguay produced a disciplined defensive display to frustrate Les Bleus for long periods.
item.content:encoded:
France have booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals after a hard-fought 1-0 win over Paraguay in Philadelphia, with Kylian Mbappé’s second-half penalty proving decisive.

The World Cup favourites were made to work for their victory as Paraguay produced a disciplined defensive display to frustrate Les Bleus for long periods.

The match remained goalless at half-time, with neither side registering a shot on target.

France finally broke the deadlock after substitute Désiré Doué was fouled in the box, with the penalty awarded following a VAR review. Mbappé then converted from the spot to seal the victory.

Paraguay, who had earlier upset Germany on penalties in the Round of 32, defended strongly throughout but were unable to overcome France’s dominance in possession.

France will face Morocco in the World Cup quarter-finals at 8am on Friday after Morocco beat Canada 3-0 earlier today.

Meanwhile, in tomorrow’s last 16 matches, Brazil will face Norway at 8am, while Mexico will take on England at 12pm.

item.title: Penalty count proves costly as Flying Fijians fall 39-24 to Wales
item.description: fijivillage , Mikaele Liga · 05/07/2026 The FIJI Water Flying Fijians' penalty count proved costly as they went down 39-24 to Wales, allowing Wales to take control of the contest.
item.content:encoded:
fijivillage , Mikaele Liga · 05/07/2026

The FIJI Water Flying Fijians' penalty count proved costly as they went down 39-24 to Wales, allowing Wales to take control of the contest.

Fiji made a bright start, putting Wales under pressure from the opening whistle and threatening the Welsh defence throughout the first half.

Former All Blacks forward Pita Gus Sowakula crossed for Fiji's opening try in just the second minute, giving the visitors an early 7-0 lead.

Wales responded in the ninth minute with their first try to narrow the margin to 7-5.

The Flying Fijians showcased their trademark flair and pace during the opening quarter, producing more than 10 offloads while Wales struggled to maintain possession.

Fiji came close to extending their lead, but winger Selestino Ravutaumada was denied a try after losing the ball over the line.

A minute later, prop Eroni Mawi also had a try ruled out after spilling the ball in the act of grounding.

Captain Tevita Ikanivere says the team wanted to play their natural brand of rugby but admitted they lacked patience with the ball.

He says they made some great line breaks, but the finishing wasn't there, and they couldn't find the right balance.

The Japan-based hooker added that it was back to the drawing board as the team prepares for its next outing.

The sides were locked at 10-10 at halftime.

Wales struck first after the break, scoring in the 43rd minute to take a 17-10 lead.

The home side capitalised on Fiji's discipline issues and maintained control throughout the second half, scoring 29 points after the break while Fiji managed 14.

Despite showing flashes of brilliance with ball in hand, the Flying Fijians were unable to overcome their errors and penalty count, eventually falling 39-24.

Fiji will now turn its attention to its next Test as it looks to bounce back from the opening defeat.

Fiji will face Scotland on July 19th in Murrayfield, Scotland.

In other results tonight, Ireland edged Australia 33-31, while All Blacks beat France 34-32.

1st minute: Wales kick off, and Fiji instantly attack the Wales line; they attack for 9 phases before losing the ball.

2nd minute: 32 seconds: Pita Gus Sowakula scores Fiji's first try to allow Fiji to take the lead 7-0.

4th minute: Wales attacks the Fijian line and passes a ball that goes forward, scrum to Fiji.

6th minute line-out to Wales, and Fiji uses the touch line to push the Wales player to the touch line; Fiji throws the ball line-out not straight; Wales ball.

9th minute Wales getting a scoring chance as they go for a line out 5 metres away from the try line, Wales scores first try through an off field decision.

12th minute Wales attack Fiji's line and Josua Tuisiva wins a turnover for Fiji.

13th minute Fiji has done 7 offloads to Wales nil.

16th minute Fiji attacks and Semi Radradra loses the ball after multiple attacks; Wales ball.

17th minute Selestino Ravutaumada loses the ball on the touchline and is awarded a penalty.

18th minute Eroni Mawi, Fiji prop, loses the ball at the score line and Wales take the scrum.

21st minute Fiji attacks and players could not control his fett, penalty to Wales.

24th minute scrum to Wales on the 22 metre line and Wales wins another penalty; they opt for a lineout.

item.title: Morocco beat Canada 3-0 to reach World Cup quarter finals
item.description: Canada's first FIFA World Cup campaign as a tournament co-host has come to an end after a 3-0 defeat to Morocco in Houston. After a goalless first half, Azzedine Ounahi gave Morocco the lead five minutes into the second half.
item.content:encoded:
Canada's first FIFA World Cup campaign as a tournament co-host has come to an end after a 3-0 defeat to Morocco in Houston.

After a goalless first half, Azzedine Ounahi gave Morocco the lead five minutes into the second half.

Ounahi scored his second with eight minutes remaining before Soufiane Rahimi added a third in stoppage time.

Morocco have advanced to the quarter finals where they will face the winner of the Round of 16 clash between France and Paraguay.

The Atlas Lions are making their seventh FIFA World Cup appearance, having first competed at the tournament in 1970.

Their greatest World Cup run came in 2022, when they became the first African team to reach the semi-finals.

Morocco eventually finished fourth at the 2022 FIFA World Cup after historic knockout-stage victories over Spain and Portugal.

Meanwhile, France and Paraguay will play at 9am.

item.title: Ba to celebrate first Fiji FACT title in 16 years with fans at Govind Park
item.description: Ba football fans will have the opportunity to celebrate the club's first BiC Fiji FACT title in 16 years when the Men-in-Black present the trophy after todays's Extra Premier League clash against Nadi at Govind Park. Fresh from ending a 16-year wait for the prestigious title, Ba will present the BiC Fiji FACT trophy to home supporters following the final whistle.
item.content:encoded:
Ba football fans will have the opportunity to celebrate the club's first BiC Fiji FACT title in 16 years when the Men-in-Black present the trophy after todays's Extra Premier League clash against Nadi at Govind Park.

Fresh from ending a 16-year wait for the prestigious title, Ba will present the BiC Fiji FACT trophy to home supporters following the final whistle.

Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says the celebration is dedicated to the loyal supporters who stood by the club through the highs and lows.

Dayal says the trophy belongs to every Ba supporter whose unwavering belief in the team has been the club's greatest strength, and Ba wants to celebrate the achievement with them.

He has urged fans to wear their black colours, get behind the team against Nadi and remain at Govind Park after the match to celebrate the historic triumph together.

management, coaching staff, players, volunteers and supporters for their commitment throughout the campaign.

Ba defeated Navua in last Sunday's BiC Fiji FACT final at Subrail Park in Labasa to claim the title for the first time since 2010.

The Extra Premier League match between Ba and Nadi will kick off at 3pm.

Meanwhile, in other matches, Nasinu FC will face Tailevu Naitasiri at 12pm, while Labasa will take on Nadroga at 2pm at Ratu Cakobau Park.

Rewa will play Lautoka at 1pm at Churchill Park while Navua will host Suva at 3pm.

item.description: Rooster Chicken Ba Women FC's hopes of reaching the semi-finals of the OFC Women's Champions League came to an end after a thrilli

item.description: Ba Women FC are leading Tafea FC of Vanuatu 3-2 at halftime in their crucial Group A clash at the OFC Women’s Champions League 202

item.title: Flying Fijians ready for historic Nations Championship opener tomorrow at 1.10am
item.description: Tomorrow, the FIJI Water Flying Fijians will begin a new chapter in their rugby history when they take on Wales in their opening N

item.title: Ba Women face crucial Tafea clash at 5pm today
item.description: Ba Women FC face a crucial final Group A match against unbeaten leaders Tafea FC of Vanuatu today, with their OFC Women's Champion

item.title: Ardie Savea urges young people to “shine your light”
item.description: All Blacks captain Ardie Savea has delivered an emotional message of encouragement to young people from underprivileged background

item.title: Argentina beat Cape Verde 3-2 after extra time to set up Egypt clash
item.description: Defending champions Argentina have survived a major scare to beat World Cup debutants Cape Verde 3-2 after extra time and reach th
